By conforming your app intent, app entity, or your app enumeration to a schema, you ensure that Apple Intelligence understands your app’s actions and content. When you’ve identified the schema to use, leverage the [doc://com.apple.AppIntents/documentation/AppIntents/AppIntent(schema:)], [doc://com.apple.AppIntents/documentation/AppIntents/AppEntity(schema:)], and [doc://com.apple.AppIntents/documentation/AppIntents/AppEnum(schema:)] macros to write schema-conforming code.

# App intent domains

**API Collection**

Make your app’s actions and content available to Siri and Apple Intelligence with assistant schemas.

## Overview

To enable enhanced understanding and more conversational interactions with Siri for your app, choose a domain and a schema that match your app’s functionality. By conforming your app intent, app entity, or your app enumeration to a schema, you ensure that Apple Intelligence understands your app’s actions and content. When you’ve identified the schema to use, leverage the [doc://com.apple.AppIntents/documentation/AppIntents/AppIntent(schema:)], [doc://com.apple.AppIntents/documentation/AppIntents/AppEntity(schema:)], and [doc://com.apple.AppIntents/documentation/AppIntents/AppEnum(schema:)] macros to write schema-conforming code.



To learn more, refer to [doc://com.apple.AppIntents/documentation/AppIntents/Integrating-actions-with-siri-and-apple-intelligence] and [doc://com.apple.AppIntents/documentation/AppIntents/Making-onscreen-content-available-to-siri-and-apple-intelligence].

## Domains

- **Assistant**: An app intent schema that lets people in Japan configure the side button of iPhone to launch your voice-based conversational app.
- **Books**: App intent schemas you use for ebook reader functionality and content.
- **Browser**: App intent schemas you use for web browsing functionality and content.
- **Camera**: App intent schemas you use for camera functionality and content.
- **File management**: App intent schemas you use for file management functionality and content.
- **Journaling**: App intent schemas you use for journaling functionality and content.
- **Mail**: App intent schemas you use for email clients.
- **Photos**: App intent schemas you use for photo and video functionality and content.
- **Presentations**: App intent schemas you use for presentation functionality and content.
- **Reader**: App intent schemas you use for document reading functionality and content.
- **Spreadsheet**: App intent schemas you use for spreadsheet functionality and content.
- **System and in-app search**: App intent schemas you use for in-app search functionality and content.
- **Visual intelligence**: An app intent schema that lets you integrate your app with visual intelligence.
- **Whiteboard**: App intent schemas you use for whiteboard functionality and content.
- **Word proccessor**: App intent schemas you use for text editing functionality and content.

## Macros

- **AppIntent(schema:)**: A Swift macro you use to make sure your app intent conforms to an schema.
- **AppEntity(schema:)**: A Swift macro you use to make sure your app entity conforms to a schema.
- **AppEnum(schema:)**: A Swift macro you use to make sure your app enum conforms to a schema.

## Base protocols

- **Assistant schema base protocols**: Protocols that provide the underlying functionality for assistant schemas.

## Deprecated macros

- **AssistantIntent(schema:)**: A Swift macro you use to make sure your app intent conforms to an assistant schema.
- **AssistantEntity(schema:)**: A Swift macro you use to make sure your app entity conforms to an assistant schema.
- **AssistantEnum(schema:)**: A Swift macro you use to make sure your app enum conforms to an assistant schema.

## Actions

- **Accelerating app interactions with App Intents**: Enable people to use your app’s features quickly through Siri, Spotlight, and Shortcuts.
- **Creating your first app intent**: Create your first app intent that makes your app available in system experiences like Spotlight or the Shortcuts app.
- **App intents**: Define the custom actions your app exposes to the system using specialized intents.
- **Intent infrastructure**: Provide supplemental context for your intents, and create infrastructure to make app intents reusable across your apps.
